title: Japanese destroyers Takanami and Imigiri shadow Chinese Liaoning CSG through Miyako Strait
summary: Fleetnumbers tracks JMSDF destroyers JS Takanami (DD-110) and JS Imigiri (DD-158) shadowing the Chinese Liaoning carrier strike group as it transited the Miyako Strait into the Philippine Sea.
contents:
# JMSDF Destroyers Shadow Liaoning CSG Through Miyako Strait

## Tracking Update
Naval fleet tracking confirms Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) destroyers JS Takanami (DD-110) and JS Imigiri (DD-158) shadowed the Chinese Liaoning carrier strike group during its transit through the Miyako Strait.

## Vessels Tracked
### Chinese Forces
- CV-16 Liaoning
- DDG-101 Nanchang (Type 055)
- DDG-131 Taiyuan (Type 052D)
- FFG-599 Anyang (Type 054A)
- DDG-104 Wuxi (Type 055) - transited separately
- DDG-121 Qiqihar (Type 052D) - transited separately

### Japanese Response
- DD-110 Takanami (Takanami-class)
- DD-158 Imigiri (Asagiri-class)
- P-3C Orion MPA from Fleet Air Wing 5, Naha
- JASDF fighters scrambled in response

## Transit Details
The Liaoning conducted 90 fixed-wing launches/recoveries and 30 helicopter operations during two days in the East China Sea before entering the Philippine Sea.

## Tags
japan, jmsdf, china, plan, liaoning, miyako-strait, naval-tracking, carrier
